title: Finding Directions in GAN's Latent Space for Neural Face Reenactment
title_zh: 在 GAN 的潜在空间中寻找神经人脸重演的方向
comment: Accepted for publication in BMVC 2022. Project page: https://stelabou.github.io/stylegan-directions-reenactment/ Code: https://github.com/StelaBou/stylegan_directions_face_reenactment
comments: Accepted for publication in BMVC 2022. Project page: https://stelabou.github.io/stylegan-directions-reenactment/ Code: https://github.com/StelaBou/stylegan_directions_face_reenactment
abstract: This paper is on face/head reenactment where the goal is to transfer the facial pose (3D head orientation and expression) of a target face to a source face. Previous methods focus on learning embedding networks for identity and pose disentanglement which proves to be a rather hard task, degrading the quality of the generated images. We take a different approach, bypassing the training of such networks, by using (fine-tuned) pre-trained GANs which have been shown capable of producing high-quality facial images. Because GANs are characterized by weak controllability, the core of our approach is a method to discover which directions in latent GAN space are responsible for controlling facial pose and expression variations. We present a simple pipeline to learn such directions with the aid of a 3D shape model which, by construction, already captures disentangled directions for facial pose, identity and expression. Moreover, we show that by embedding real images in the GAN latent space, our method can be successfully used for the reenactment of real-world faces. Our method features several favorable properties including using a single source image (one-shot) and enabling cross-person reenactment. Our qualitative and quantitative results show that our approach often produces reenacted faces of significantly higher quality than those produced by state-of-the-art methods for the standard benchmarks of VoxCeleb1 & 2. Source code is available at: https://github.com/StelaBou/stylegan_directions_face_reenactment
summary: This paper is on face/head reenactment where the goal is to transfer the facial pose (3D head orientation and expression) of a target face to a source face. Previous methods focus on learning embedding networks for identity and pose disentanglement which proves to be a rather hard task, degrading the quality of the generated images. We take a different approach, bypassing the training of such networks, by using (fine-tuned) pre-trained GANs which have been shown capable of producing high-quality facial images. Because GANs are characterized by weak controllability, the core of our approach is a method to discover which directions in latent GAN space are responsible for controlling facial pose and expression variations. We present a simple pipeline to learn such directions with the aid of a 3D shape model which, by construction, already captures disentangled directions for facial pose, identity and expression. Moreover, we show that by embedding real images in the GAN latent space, our method can be successfully used for the reenactment of real-world faces. Our method features several favorable properties including using a single source image (one-shot) and enabling cross-person reenactment. Our qualitative and quantitative results show that our approach often produces reenacted faces of significantly higher quality than those produced by state-of-the-art methods for the standard benchmarks of VoxCeleb1 & 2. Source code is available at: https://github.com/StelaBou/stylegan_directions_face_reenactment
abstract_zh: 本文涉及面部/头部重演，其目标是将目标面部的面部姿势（3D 头部方向和表情）转移到源面部。以前的方法侧重于学习嵌入网络以实现身份和姿态解开，这被证明是一项相当艰巨的任务，降低了生成图像的质量。我们采用不同的方法，通过使用（微调的）预训练的 GAN 来绕过此类网络的训练，这些 GAN 已被证明能够生成高质量的面部图像。由于 GAN 的特点是可控性较弱，因此我们方法的核心是发现潜在 GAN 空间中的哪些方向负责控制面部姿势和表情变化的方法。我们提出了一个简单的管道，借助 3D 形状模型来学习这些方向，通过构建该模型，已经捕获了面部姿势、身份和表情的解开方向。此外，我们还表明，通过在 GAN 潜在空间中嵌入真实图像，我们的方法可以成功地用于重现真实世界的面孔。我们的方法具有几个有利的特性，包括使用单个源图像（一次性）和实现跨人员重演。我们的定性和定量结果表明，我们的方法通常会生成比 VoxCeleb1 和 2 标准基准的最先进方法生成的质量明显更高的重演面孔。源代码位于：https://github.com/StelaBou/stylegan_directions_face_reenactment

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于预训练GAN的神经人脸重演方法，通过在潜在空间中寻找控制面部姿态和表情的方向，实现将目标人脸的面部姿态和表情迁移到源人脸。
AI.motivation: 传统方法通过训练嵌入网络实现身份和姿态解耦，但效果不佳。预训练GAN能生成高质量人脸，但控制性较弱，需探索潜在空间中控制姿态和表情的方向。
AI.method: 利用预训练GAN并结合3D形状模型，通过学习潜在空间中与姿态、表情相关的方向，构建简单流水线实现单张源图像的跨人重演。
AI.result: 在VoxCeleb1&2基准测试中，生成质量显著优于现有方法，支持单次拍摄和跨人重演。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过避免复杂网络训练，直接利用预训练GAN潜在空间方向，实现了高效高质量的人脸重演。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于预训练GAN的神经人脸重演方法，通过在潜在空间中寻找控制面部姿态和表情的方向，实现将目标人脸的面部姿态和表情迁移到源人脸。
ai_content.motivation: 传统方法通过训练嵌入网络实现身份和姿态解耦，但效果不佳。预训练GAN能生成高质量人脸，但控制性较弱，需探索潜在空间中控制姿态和表情的方向。
ai_content.method: 利用预训练GAN并结合3D形状模型，通过学习潜在空间中与姿态、表情相关的方向，构建简单流水线实现单张源图像的跨人重演。
ai_content.result: 在VoxCeleb1&2基准测试中，生成质量显著优于现有方法，支持单次拍摄和跨人重演。
ai_content.conclusion: 该方法通过避免复杂网络训练，直接利用预训练GAN潜在空间方向，实现了高效高质量的人脸重演。

search_text: Finding Directions in GAN's Latent Space for Neural Face Reenactment 在 GAN 的潜在空间中寻找神经人脸重演的方向 This paper is on face/head reenactment where the goal is to transfer the facial pose (3D head orientation and expression) of a target face to a source face. Previous methods focus on learning embedding networks for identity and pose disentanglement which proves to be a rather hard task, degrading the quality of the generated images. We take a different approach, bypassing the training of such networks, by using (fine-tuned) pre-trained GANs which have been shown capable of producing high-quality facial images. Because GANs are characterized by weak controllability, the core of our approach is a method to discover which directions in latent GAN space are responsible for controlling facial pose and expression variations. We present a simple pipeline to learn such directions with the aid of a 3D shape model which, by construction, already captures disentangled directions for facial pose, identity and expression. Moreover, we show that by embedding real images in the GAN latent space, our method can be successfully used for the reenactment of real-world faces. Our method features several favorable properties including using a single source image (one-shot) and enabling cross-person reenactment. Our qualitative and quantitative results show that our approach often produces reenacted faces of significantly higher quality than those produced by state-of-the-art methods for the standard benchmarks of VoxCeleb1 & 2. Source code is available at: https://github.com/StelaBou/stylegan_directions_face_reenactment 本文涉及面部/头部重演，其目标是将目标面部的面部姿势（3D 头部方向和表情）转移到源面部。以前的方法侧重于学习嵌入网络以实现身份和姿态解开，这被证明是一项相当艰巨的任务，降低了生成图像的质量。我们采用不同的方法，通过使用（微调的）预训练的 GAN 来绕过此类网络的训练，这些 GAN 已被证明能够生成高质量的面部图像。由于 GAN 的特点是可控性较弱，因此我们方法的核心是发现潜在 GAN 空间中的哪些方向负责控制面部姿势和表情变化的方法。我们提出了一个简单的管道，借助 3D 形状模型来学习这些方向，通过构建该模型，已经捕获了面部姿势、身份和表情的解开方向。此外，我们还表明，通过在 GAN 潜在空间中嵌入真实图像，我们的方法可以成功地用于重现真实世界的面孔。我们的方法具有几个有利的特性，包括使用单个源图像（一次性）和实现跨人员重演。我们的定性和定量结果表明，我们的方法通常会生成比 VoxCeleb1 和 2 标准基准的最先进方法生成的质量明显更高的重演面孔。源代码位于：https://github.com/StelaBou/stylegan_directions_face_reenactment 本文提出一种基于预训练GAN的神经人脸重演方法，通过在潜在空间中寻找控制面部姿态和表情的方向，实现将目标人脸的面部姿态和表情迁移到源人脸。 Stella Bounareli,Vasileios Argyriou,Georgios Tzimiropoulos cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Max Ehrlich,Jon Barker,Namitha Padmanabhan,Larry Davis,Andrew Tao,Bryan Catanzaro,Abhinav Shrivastava
title: Leveraging Bitstream Metadata for Fast, Accurate, Generalized Compressed Video Quality Enhancement
title_zh: 利用比特流元数据快速、准确、通用地增强压缩视频质量
abstract: Video compression is a central feature of the modern internet powering technologies from social media to video conferencing. While video compression continues to mature, for many compression settings, quality loss is still noticeable. These settings nevertheless have important applications to the efficient transmission of videos over bandwidth constrained or otherwise unstable connections. In this work, we develop a deep learning architecture capable of restoring detail to compressed videos which leverages the underlying structure and motion information embedded in the video bitstream. We show that this improves restoration accuracy compared to prior compression correction methods and is competitive when compared with recent deep-learning-based video compression methods on rate-distortion while achieving higher throughput. Furthermore, we condition our model on quantization data which is readily available in the bitstream. This allows our single model to handle a variety of different compression quality settings which required an ensemble of models in prior work.
summary: Video compression is a central feature of the modern internet powering technologies from social media to video conferencing. While video compression continues to mature, for many compression settings, quality loss is still noticeable. These settings nevertheless have important applications to the efficient transmission of videos over bandwidth constrained or otherwise unstable connections. In this work, we develop a deep learning architecture capable of restoring detail to compressed videos which leverages the underlying structure and motion information embedded in the video bitstream. We show that this improves restoration accuracy compared to prior compression correction methods and is competitive when compared with recent deep-learning-based video compression methods on rate-distortion while achieving higher throughput. Furthermore, we condition our model on quantization data which is readily available in the bitstream. This allows our single model to handle a variety of different compression quality settings which required an ensemble of models in prior work.
abstract_zh: 视频压缩是从社交媒体到视频会议的现代互联网驱动技术的核心特征。尽管视频压缩不断成熟，但对于许多压缩设置来说，质量损失仍然很明显。然而，这些设置对于通过带宽受限或其他不稳定连接有效传输视频具有重要的应用。在这项工作中，我们开发了一种深度学习架构，能够利用视频比特流中嵌入的底层结构和运动信息来恢复压缩视频的细节。我们表明，与之前的压缩校正方法相比，这提高了恢复精度，并且与最近基于深度学习的视频压缩方法在率失真方面具有竞争力，同时实现了更高的吞吐量。此外，我们根据比特流中容易获得的量化数据来调整我们的模型。这使得我们的单一模型能够处理各种不同的压缩质量设置，这在之前的工作中需要模型的集合。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于视频比特流元数据的深度学习方法，用于提升压缩视频的质量。
AI.motivation: 视频压缩在现代互联网中至关重要，但压缩质量损失在带宽受限场景下仍显著，需高效准确的增强方法。
AI.method: 开发深度学习架构，利用比特流中的结构和运动信息，并基于量化数据条件建模以适应不同压缩质量。
AI.result: 相比传统方法提升修复精度，在率失真性能上与最新方法相当且吞吐量更高，单模型适配多种压缩设置。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过利用比特流元数据和条件建模，在压缩视频增强任务中实现了精度与通用性的平衡。

title: MEGA: Model Stealing via Collaborative Generator-Substitute Networks
title_zh: MEGA：通过协作生成器替代网络进行模型窃取
abstract: Deep machine learning models are increasingly deployedin the wild for providing services to users. Adversaries maysteal the knowledge of these valuable models by trainingsubstitute models according to the inference results of thetargeted deployed models. Recent data-free model stealingmethods are shown effective to extract the knowledge of thetarget model without using real query examples, but they as-sume rich inference information, e.g., class probabilities andlogits. However, they are all based on competing generator-substitute networks and hence encounter training instability.In this paper we propose a data-free model stealing frame-work,MEGA, which is based on collaborative generator-substitute networks and only requires the target model toprovide label prediction for synthetic query examples. Thecore of our method is a model stealing optimization con-sisting of two collaborative models (i) the substitute modelwhich imitates the target model through the synthetic queryexamples and their inferred labels and (ii) the generatorwhich synthesizes images such that the confidence of thesubstitute model over each query example is maximized. Wepropose a novel coordinate descent training procedure andanalyze its convergence. We also empirically evaluate thetrained substitute model on three datasets and its applicationon black-box adversarial attacks. Our results show that theaccuracy of our trained substitute model and the adversarialattack success rate over it can be up to 33% and 40% higherthan state-of-the-art data-free black-box attacks.
summary: Deep machine learning models are increasingly deployedin the wild for providing services to users. Adversaries maysteal the knowledge of these valuable models by trainingsubstitute models according to the inference results of thetargeted deployed models. Recent data-free model stealingmethods are shown effective to extract the knowledge of thetarget model without using real query examples, but they as-sume rich inference information, e.g., class probabilities andlogits. However, they are all based on competing generator-substitute networks and hence encounter training instability.In this paper we propose a data-free model stealing frame-work,MEGA, which is based on collaborative generator-substitute networks and only requires the target model toprovide label prediction for synthetic query examples. Thecore of our method is a model stealing optimization con-sisting of two collaborative models (i) the substitute modelwhich imitates the target model through the synthetic queryexamples and their inferred labels and (ii) the generatorwhich synthesizes images such that the confidence of thesubstitute model over each query example is maximized. Wepropose a novel coordinate descent training procedure andanalyze its convergence. We also empirically evaluate thetrained substitute model on three datasets and its applicationon black-box adversarial attacks. Our results show that theaccuracy of our trained substitute model and the adversarialattack success rate over it can be up to 33% and 40% higherthan state-of-the-art data-free black-box attacks.
abstract_zh: 深度机器学习模型越来越多地在野外部署来为用户提供服务。对手可以通过根据目标部署模型的推理结果训练替代模型来窃取这些有价值的模型的知识。最近的无数据模型窃取方法被证明可以有效地提取目标模型的知识，而无需使用真实的查询示例，但它们假设丰富的推理信息，例如类概率和逻辑。然而，它们都是基于竞争的生成器替代网络，因此会遇到训练不稳定的问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种无数据模型窃取框架MEGA，它基于协作生成器替代网络，只需要目标模型为合成查询示例提供标签预测。我们方法的核心是模型窃取优化，由两个协作模型组成（i）通过合成查询示例及其推断标签来模仿目标模型的替代模型，以及（ii）合成图像的生成器，以使替代模型对每个查询示例的置信度最大化。我们提出了一种新颖的坐标下降训练程序并分析了其收敛性。我们还实证评估了三个数据集上经过训练的替代模型及其在黑盒对抗攻击中的应用。我们的结果表明，我们训练的替代模型的准确性和对抗性攻击的成功率比最先进的无数据黑盒攻击高出 33% 和 40%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为MEGA的数据无关模型窃取框架，通过协作生成器-替代网络从目标模型的标签预测中提取知识。
AI.motivation: 现有数据无关模型窃取方法依赖目标模型的丰富推理信息（如概率和logits），且基于竞争性网络导致训练不稳定，需改进稳定性与效率。
AI.method: 设计协作生成器-替代网络框架，包含替代模型（通过合成数据模仿目标模型）和生成器（最大化替代模型置信度），并提出坐标下降训练法保证收敛。
AI.result: 在三个数据集上验证，替代模型准确率比现有方法高33%，对抗攻击成功率提升40%。
AI.conclusion: MEGA通过协作优化机制实现了更高效稳定的模型窃取，显著优于现有数据无关黑盒攻击方法。

Wepropose a novel coordinate descent training procedure andanalyze its convergence. We also empirically evaluate thetrained substitute model on three datasets and its applicationon black-box adversarial attacks. Our results show that theaccuracy of our trained substitute model and the adversarialattack success rate over it can be up to 33% and 40% higherthan state-of-the-art data-free black-box attacks. 深度机器学习模型越来越多地在野外部署来为用户提供服务。对手可以通过根据目标部署模型的推理结果训练替代模型来窃取这些有价值的模型的知识。最近的无数据模型窃取方法被证明可以有效地提取目标模型的知识，而无需使用真实的查询示例，但它们假设丰富的推理信息，例如类概率和逻辑。然而，它们都是基于竞争的生成器替代网络，因此会遇到训练不稳定的问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种无数据模型窃取框架MEGA，它基于协作生成器替代网络，只需要目标模型为合成查询示例提供标签预测。我们方法的核心是模型窃取优化，由两个协作模型组成（i）通过合成查询示例及其推断标签来模仿目标模型的替代模型，以及（ii）合成图像的生成器，以使替代模型对每个查询示例的置信度最大化。我们提出了一种新颖的坐标下降训练程序并分析了其收敛性。我们还实证评估了三个数据集上经过训练的替代模型及其在黑盒对抗攻击中的应用。我们的结果表明，我们训练的替代模型的准确性和对抗性攻击的成功率比最先进的无数据黑盒攻击高出 33% 和 40%。 本文提出了一种名为MEGA的数据无关模型窃取框架，通过协作生成器-替代网络从目标模型的标签预测中提取知识。 Chi Hong,Jiyue Huang,Lydia Y. Chen cs.CR,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Weihao Yu,Hao Zheng,Minghui Zhang,Hanxiao Zhang,Jiayuan Sun,Jie Yang
title: BREAK: Bronchi Reconstruction by gEodesic transformation And sKeleton embedding
title_zh: BREAK：通过测地线变换和骨架嵌入进行支气管重建
abstract: Airway segmentation is critical for virtual bronchoscopy and computer-aided pulmonary disease analysis. In recent years, convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have been widely used to delineate the bronchial tree. However, the segmentation results of the CNN-based methods usually include many discontinuous branches, which need manual repair in clinical use. A major reason for the breakages is that the appearance of the airway wall can be affected by the lung disease as well as the adjacency of the vessels, while the network tends to overfit to these special patterns in the training set. To learn robust features for these areas, we design a multi-branch framework that adopts the geodesic distance transform to capture the intensity changes between airway lumen and wall. Another reason for the breakages is the intra-class imbalance. Since the volume of the peripheral bronchi may be much smaller than the large branches in an input patch, the common segmentation loss is not sensitive to the breakages among the distal branches. Therefore, in this paper, a breakage-sensitive regularization term is designed and can be easily combined with other loss functions. Extensive experiments are conducted on publicly available datasets. Compared with state-of-the-art methods, our framework can detect more branches while maintaining competitive segmentation performance.
summary: Airway segmentation is critical for virtual bronchoscopy and computer-aided pulmonary disease analysis. In recent years, convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have been widely used to delineate the bronchial tree. However, the segmentation results of the CNN-based methods usually include many discontinuous branches, which need manual repair in clinical use. A major reason for the breakages is that the appearance of the airway wall can be affected by the lung disease as well as the adjacency of the vessels, while the network tends to overfit to these special patterns in the training set. To learn robust features for these areas, we design a multi-branch framework that adopts the geodesic distance transform to capture the intensity changes between airway lumen and wall. Another reason for the breakages is the intra-class imbalance. Since the volume of the peripheral bronchi may be much smaller than the large branches in an input patch, the common segmentation loss is not sensitive to the breakages among the distal branches. Therefore, in this paper, a breakage-sensitive regularization term is designed and can be easily combined with other loss functions. Extensive experiments are conducted on publicly available datasets. Compared with state-of-the-art methods, our framework can detect more branches while maintaining competitive segmentation performance.
abstract_zh: 气道分割对于虚拟支气管镜检查和计算机辅助肺部疾病分析至关重要。近年来，卷积神经网络（CNN）已被广泛用于描绘支气管树。然而，基于CNN的方法的分割结果通常包含许多不连续的分支，在临床使用中需要手动修复。破损的主要原因是气道壁的外观可能受到肺部疾病以及血管邻近性的影响，而网络往往会过度拟合训练集中的这些特殊模式。为了了解这些区域的鲁棒特征，我们设计了一个多分支框架，采用测地距离变换来捕获气道腔和壁之间的强度变化。造成破裂的另一个原因是班级内部的不平衡。由于外周支气管的体积可能比输入斑块中的大分支小得多，因此常见的分割损失对远端分支之间的断裂不敏感。因此，本文设计了一种破损敏感正则化项，并且可以很容易地与其他损失函数结合。在公开数据集上进行了广泛的实验。与最先进的方法相比，我们的框架可以检测更多分支，同时保持有竞争力的分割性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出BREAK框架，通过改进分割方法解决气道分割中的断裂问题，提升虚拟支气管镜和肺部疾病分析的准确性。
AI.motivation: 基于CNN的气道分割方法易因疾病影响和血管邻近导致分割断裂，且传统损失函数对远端小支气管断裂不敏感，需设计更鲁棒的解决方案。
AI.method: 设计多分支框架，结合测地变换捕捉气道腔室与壁的强度变化，并引入断裂敏感正则化项优化损失函数，增强对小支气管的分割能力。
AI.result: 在公开数据集上实验表明，该方法能检测更多支气管分支，同时保持与现有方法相当的分割性能。
AI.conclusion: BREAK框架通过几何特征建模与损失函数改进，有效缓解了气道分割断裂问题，为临床肺部疾病分析提供更可靠的工具。
